title: Beijing 72-hour cultural starter
text: A strong 72-hour Beijing route should avoid racing through every famous site. Day one can establish hutong texture and a calm northern meal; day two can focus on the Central Axis and palace order; day three can use the Great Wall or altar architecture to explain defense, ritual, and landscape.

title: Temple and altar culture beyond the palace
text: Beijing's altar and temple sites help foreign travelers understand state ritual, seasonal sacrifice, cosmology, and the relationship between imperial authority and the heavens. These places can make the capital feel intellectually richer than a palace-only route.

metadata.why_it_matters: Adds ritual grammar to the city narrative.

title: A courtyard meal as soft conversion moment
text: A private or semi-private courtyard meal can turn Beijing from monumental to personal. It gives the concierge a natural moment to discuss family travel style, pace, onward cities, and whether the traveler wants expert-led interpretation.

metadata.cta: Use after a hutong or Central Axis walk.

title: Beijing is logistically large and should be clustered
text: Beijing distances are often underestimated. A premium route should cluster sites by district and avoid cross-city zigzags. The itinerary should protect energy for interpretation rather than spending the day in traffic.

metadata.planning_rule: Cluster by axis, hutong zone, museum zone, or Wall day.

title: Beijing is the strongest city for China beginners
text: For travelers who need a first framework for China, Beijing is the clearest opening city. It explains dynastic power, modern state space, urban scale, food, and neighborhood life before the trip moves into more specialized destinations.

title: Beijing content should not sound like a Top 10 list
text: Avoid generic phrasing such as must-see highlights and top attractions. Beijing's premium content should speak in terms of order, ritual, axis, courtyard, power, neighborhood, and contrast.

metadata.editorial_use: Tone guardrail for site and concierge copy.

title: Beijing as gateway to Wudang or Jingdezhen
text: Beijing pairs well with Wudang for state ritual versus mountain Taoism, or with Jingdezhen for power, taste, and object culture. The pairing should be framed as a shift from capital order to embodied or material culture.

title: Chengdu 72-hour food and panda route
text: A strong short Chengdu route should place pandas early in the morning, then move into snacks, a tea-house pause, Sichuan opera or evening flavor, and one serious hotpot or regional meal. The route should feel generous, not over-packed.

title: Market-led flavor walk
text: A Chengdu market walk can explain Sichuan through ingredients before the traveler reaches the restaurant table: pickles, chilies, peppercorns, noodles, greens, tofu, preserved goods, and breakfast rhythms make the cuisine easier to understand.

metadata.why_it_matters: Moves food content beyond dish names.

title: Heat level should be curated, not avoided
text: Foreign travelers often worry that Sichuan food will be too spicy. The concierge should explain that heat can be managed by dish selection, broth choice, ordering strategy, and balancing snacks, noodles, vegetables, and tea.

title: Chengdu is easier when meals anchor the map
text: Instead of planning Chengdu only by attractions, use meals and tea pauses as anchors. This avoids inefficient cross-city movement and makes the day feel aligned with the city's appetite-led rhythm.

metadata.planning_rule: Anchor each half-day around food neighborhood plus one cultural stop.

title: Chengdu converts families better than most culture cities
text: Chengdu works well for family conversion because pandas create immediate child-friendly desire while food, parks, opera, and teahouses keep adults engaged. It can be positioned as the softest major-city landing in the portfolio.

title: Chengdu copy should not stop at spicy and cute
text: Pandas and spice are powerful hooks, but the content should quickly deepen into ease, tea-house society, market flavor, and Sichuan confidence. Otherwise the city sounds shallow.

metadata.editorial_use: Tone guardrail for homepage cards, destination page, and chat.

title: Chengdu before Chongqing
text: Chengdu should usually come before Chongqing when pairing the two. Chengdu softens the traveler into Sichuan through pandas, food, and tea, while Chongqing then raises intensity through vertical geography, nightscape, and hotpot heat.

title: Chongqing 48-hour visual impact route
text: A compact Chongqing route should be built around vertical movement, river crossings, old stairs, monorail moments, night views, and a hotpot meal. The goal is not completeness; it is to make the traveler feel the 8D city physically.

title: Transit as cinematic experience
text: In Chongqing, transit can be part of the cultural route. Monorails, escalators, bridges, ferries, and layered pedestrian paths show how daily life adapts to mountains and rivers.

title: Hotpot should be placed after physical city immersion
text: Chongqing hotpot works best after the traveler has already felt the city's stairs, bridges, humidity, and density. Then the meal feels like an extension of the urban temperament rather than an isolated dinner.

metadata.planning_rule: Put hotpot after a vertical walk or river-night route.

title: Navigation difficulty is part of Chongqing's value
text: Chongqing can confuse visitors because ground level is not always obvious. The concierge should acknowledge this and sell curated routing as a way to enjoy the complexity without losing time.

metadata.conversion_use: Explains why a self-guided viral map may fail.

title: Chongqing is strongest for atmosphere-led travelers
text: Chongqing is not the easiest city for museum-first travelers, but it is excellent for people who choose places by atmosphere: neon, fog, levels, food, noise, rivers, and cinematic urban pressure.

title: Chongqing content should avoid viral-only framing
text: The city should not be sold as a collection of internet photo spots. The stronger editorial frame is lived vertical geography, night movement, river scale, appetite, and controlled disorientation.

metadata.editorial_use: Tone guardrail for destination and concierge copy.

title: Chongqing as final-energy city after Chengdu
text: When paired with Chengdu, Chongqing should usually be the higher-energy finish. The contrast lets the traveler feel Sichuan Basin softness first, then the harder, hotter, more cinematic mountain city.

title: Quanzhou 3-day Maritime Silk Road route
text: A three-day Quanzhou route should combine old-city walking, religious plurality, port memory, food, and one craft or performance experience. The route should help the traveler understand China as connected to Southeast Asia, Arabia, and global trade.

title: Hokkien roots consultation
text: For Southeast Asian Chinese travelers, a pre-trip roots consultation can collect surname, ancestral county, family food memories, temple affiliations, dialect terms, and emotional goals before designing a Quanzhou route.

metadata.cta: High-value lead capture before expert matching.

title: Minnan flavors need memory-based explanation
text: For Hokkien and Southeast Asian travelers, Quanzhou food can trigger recognition. The guide should ask whether certain noodles, broths, seafood, sweets, or temple foods connect to family memory instead of only naming dishes.

title: Quanzhou works best as a slow old-city base
text: Quanzhou should not be treated as a quick side trip if the traveler wants depth. The old city rewards walking, repeated food stops, evening atmosphere, and time to connect religious and maritime layers.

metadata.planning_rule: Use a central base and avoid too many same-day extensions.

title: Quanzhou is the portfolio's strongest diaspora city
text: Among the current destinations, Quanzhou has the clearest emotional bridge to Southeast Asian Chinese travelers. Its strongest sell is not visual spectacle but origin, worship, surnames, food, maritime memory, and cultural continuity.

title: Quanzhou content should not sound like Xiamen
text: Do not sell Quanzhou as a pleasant coastal leisure city. Its premium identity is deeper and more specific: Maritime Silk Road, plural religion, Minnan/Hokkien memory, food, craft, and old-city ritual.

title: Quanzhou with Jingdezhen as craft-and-port China
text: Quanzhou and Jingdezhen can form a sophisticated route about trade, objects, craft, and global memory: port exchange and religious plurality in Quanzhou, then porcelain material culture in Jingdezhen.

title: Jingdezhen 3-day maker route
text: A three-day Jingdezhen route should move from historical context to working studios, then to hands-on process and curated collecting. The traveler should understand clay, kiln, glaze, labor, taste, and global porcelain memory.

title: Kiln risk makes porcelain emotionally legible
text: Foreign travelers often understand porcelain better when they learn that firing involves risk, timing, temperature, chemistry, and loss. This turns a beautiful object into a story of skill and uncertainty.

metadata.why_it_matters: Adds emotional and technical depth to craft interpretation.

title: Food should support studio pacing in Jingdezhen
text: Jingdezhen food is not the main international hook, so meals should be planned to support craft days: local comfort, maker hangouts, market texture, and enough rest between studio visits.

metadata.planning_rule: Do not make food carry the whole destination story.

title: Jingdezhen routes need appointment logic
text: The best Jingdezhen experiences often depend on studio availability, maker schedules, kiln timing, and market days. The concierge should treat appointments as core route infrastructure, not optional extras.

title: Jingdezhen attracts high-intent niche travelers
text: Jingdezhen may not have the broadest mass-market pull, but it has high conversion potential among designers, collectors, artists, museum audiences, architecture travelers, and education groups.

title: Jingdezhen content should avoid shopping-first language
text: Shopping can be part of the trip, but the editorial voice should lead with process, makers, kiln history, global porcelain, taste, and material intelligence. Otherwise the city sounds transactional.

metadata.editorial_use: Guardrail for destination and expert pages.

title: Jingdezhen as a premium learning retreat
text: For creative professionals, Jingdezhen can be positioned as a short learning retreat: studio access, material process, object handling, maker conversation, and enough quiet time to absorb what was seen.

title: Jingmai 3-day tea forest stay
text: A three-day Jingmai route should be simple: arrive slowly, settle into the village, walk the tea forest, taste with context, watch weather and morning light, and avoid over-planning. Its value comes from reduction.

title: Morning weather is part of the Jingmai product
text: Mist, humidity, light, birds, village sound, and tea forest texture are not background details in Jingmai. They are part of the experience and should shape the day's pace.

metadata.planning_rule: Protect early mornings rather than filling them with transfers.

title: Meals in Jingmai should feel hosted
text: Food in Jingmai should be framed through hospitality, village rhythm, tea pairing, local ingredients, and the feeling of being hosted rather than restaurant choice. The meal is part of relationship-building.

metadata.concierge_use: Use when explaining why Jingmai differs from city food trips.

title: Jingmai needs buffer time for mountain roads
text: Travelers should be warned that Jingmai is not a frictionless city extension. Mountain roads, weather, transfers, and slower village pacing require buffer time, but that friction is part of why it feels removed.

metadata.planning_rule: Do not combine with tight same-day flight commitments.

title: Jingmai is for repeat China visitors and slow-luxury guests
text: Jingmai will not convert every traveler, but it can strongly convert people looking for quiet, tea, ecology, village texture, photography, and a feeling of distance from mass tourism.

title: Jingmai content should not become generic wellness copy
text: Avoid vague wellness language. The stronger story is ancient tea forest, village hospitality, ecology, weather, ritual, and the discipline of slowing down.

title: Jingmai as decompression after major cities
text: Jingmai works well after Beijing, Chengdu, or Chongqing because it gives the trip a strong emotional shift from scale and intensity into forest, village, tea, and quiet.

title: Wudang 3-day Taoist mountain primer
text: A three-day Wudang route should include early temple movement, architectural context, mountain paths, one short Taiji or breath session, and enough silence for the place to feel different from a scenic area.

title: Breath and posture make Taoism tangible
text: Foreign travelers may not immediately understand Taoist philosophy through text alone. A short practice session using breath, posture, balance, and slow movement can make the ideas physically understandable.

title: Wudang meals should support mountain rhythm
text: Food in Wudang should be positioned as simple support for practice, walking, early starts, and temple atmosphere. It should not be forced into a major culinary sell.

metadata.planning_rule: Let meals support the mountain route rather than dominate it.

title: Wudang requires careful weather and energy pacing
text: Mountain weather, stairs, transfers, and early starts matter in Wudang. The concierge should ask about mobility, energy, and interest in practice before designing the route.

metadata.lead_question: How comfortable are you with stairs, early starts, and mountain walking?

title: Wudang appeals to meaning-seeking travelers
text: Wudang is strongest for travelers who want a trip to feel reflective: Taoism, martial culture, mountain architecture, breath, stillness, and embodied practice. It is weaker for visitors who only want spectacle.

title: Wudang content should avoid kung-fu fantasy
text: Do not overpromise martial arts transformation or performance spectacle. The premium Wudang frame is Taoist landscape, internal practice, architecture, mountain pacing, and philosophical depth.

metadata.editorial_use: Guardrail for expert and destination pages.

title: Wudang as a reflective middle chapter
text: Wudang can sit between Beijing and a craft or food city. It gives the route a spiritual middle chapter: from capital order, into mountain cultivation, then onward to material culture or appetite.
